info.title: kiapi DepthPro API
info.description:
Monocular depth estimation from a single image (Apple Depth Pro via mflux).

This is **image-to-depth**, not prompt-driven image generation: you give one
image and get back a metric depth map.

## Upstream docs
- [mflux](https://github.com/filipstrand/mflux) — the MLX engine kiapi runs
- [apple/DepthPro](https://huggingface.co/apple/DepthPro) — model weights
- [apple/ml-depth-pro](https://github.com/apple/ml-depth-pro) — original Depth Pro code and paper

## What you get back
- A grayscale depth **PNG** where brighter pixels are nearer and darker pixels
  are farther — ready to display or overlay.
- Optionally (default on, `include_depth_data=true`) a compressed **NPZ**
  containing the raw float depth array plus `min_depth` / `max_depth` in meters,
  for downstream numeric use (point clouds, masking, measurement).

The depth array is loadable with NumPy: `np.load(path)["depth"]`, with scalar
`min_depth` / `max_depth` also stored in the same archive.

## Models
- **base** (default) — `apple/DepthPro`. The only variant today.

## Quantization
`quantize` selects the model's bit-width (one of 3, 4, 5, 6, 8; server default
8). The warmed, resident model uses the server default; requesting a *different*
`quantize` builds a one-off transient model for that call, which loads weights
fresh and is therefore slower and not reused. Keep `quantize` unset (or equal to
the default) for the fast resident path.

## TIPS
- For a quick visual, leave `include_depth_data=false` and call `sync` — you get
  the PNG bytes straight back (`curl -o depth.png`).
- For measurement or 3D work, keep `include_depth_data=true` and read the NPZ.
- Large uploads are bounded by an input pixel cap; downscale very large images
  before sending. Depth Pro resizes internally, so modest inputs are fine.
- First use downloads Apple's Depth Pro weights; run `kiapi activate` ahead of
  time to avoid a cold-start download on the first request.

post.description:
Estimate a depth map from one image (Depth Pro, image-to-depth).

Produces a grayscale depth PNG (near = bright, far = dark) and, unless
`include_depth_data` is false, a compressed NPZ holding the raw float depth
array plus min/max depth. The same endpoint serves both `sync` and `async`
via `mode`.

Sync content negotiation: when exactly one artifact is produced (i.e.
`include_depth_data=false`) and the client does not ask for JSON, the raw PNG
bytes are returned with `X-Kiapi-File-Id` / `X-Kiapi-Job-Id` headers — so
`curl -o depth.png` just works. Otherwise (JSON requested, two artifacts, or
async) the Job JSON is returned, whose `result` follows EstimateResponse.

A `quantize` value differing from the resident model runs a one-off transient
model (slower, not reused). Async returns 202 immediately; poll
GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} and fetch artifacts via GET /v1/files/{file_id}.

parameters.name: Accept

schema.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.
schema.title: Accept

parameters.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.

200.description: Sync result. Returns Job JSON with Accept: application/json; single-artifact jobs may return raw bytes otherwise.

X-Kiapi-File-Id.description: Produced artifact file_id when raw bytes are returned.

X-Kiapi-Job-Id.description: Job id when raw bytes are returned.

400.description: Invalid request for the selected model or file reference.

503.description: Model setup or memory budget error.

504.description: Sync request exceeded the configured timeout.

get.description:
List the servable models for this capability.

Returns the public catalog of every variant selectable via the ``model``
field on this capability's endpoints.

schema.title: Response List Models V1 Image Depthpro Models Get

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: In-memory job id. Poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} to inspect status, progress, result, and artifacts.

type.title: Type
type.description: Job type. Generation APIs use values such as zimage, flux2-edit, or acestep-extract.

status.description: Initial job status. Async responses are normally queued unless the worker starts immediately.

name.title: Name
name.description: Model variant name to pass in the request model field.

family.title: Family
family.description: Capability family that resolves this model variant.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Capability domain used for grouping model lists.

aliases.title: Aliases
aliases.description: Alternative names that also resolve to this model.

default.title: Default
default.description: Whether this is the default model when the request omits model.

features.title: Features
features.description: Handler-declared modalities and features supported by this model.

CapabilityModelSpec.title: CapabilityModelSpec
CapabilityModelSpec.description: Public model discovery entry for capability-specific model lists.

model.title: Model
model.description: Model variant (see GET /v1/image/depthpro/models). Omit for the default `base`. Aliases `depthpro`, `depth-pro`, `DepthPro` are accepted.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: `sync` waits for the depth map (504 on timeout); `async` returns 202 with a job_id immediately — poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}.

image.description: Input image to estimate depth for, as a Files-API file id, http(s) URL, or data URL. Must not exceed the configured input pixel cap.

quantize.title: Quantize
quantize.description: Quantization bits, one of {3, 4, 5, 6, 8}. Omit to use the server default (8). A value differing from the resident model's quantization runs a one-off transient model (no resident reuse), so it is slower.

include_depth_data.title: Include Depth Data
include_depth_data.description: When true, also store a compressed NPZ with the raw float depth array and min/max depth (`depth_data_file_id`) alongside the grayscale PNG. When false, only the PNG is produced, so a sync call can return the raw PNG bytes directly (single artifact, unless Accept: application/json).

model.title: Model
model.description: Resolved model variant that produced the depth map.

depth_image_file_id.title: Depth Image File Id
depth_image_file_id.description: Files-API id of the grayscale depth PNG (near = bright, far = dark). Fetch metadata at GET /v1/files/{id} or bytes at /download. This is also the artifact returned as raw bytes by a single-artifact sync call.

depth_image_bytes.title: Depth Image Bytes
depth_image_bytes.description: Size of the depth PNG in bytes.

depth_data_file_id.title: Depth Data File Id
depth_data_file_id.description: Files-API id of the compressed NPZ holding the raw float depth array plus min/max depth, or null when `include_depth_data` was false.

depth_data_bytes.title: Depth Data Bytes
depth_data_bytes.description: Size of the NPZ in bytes, or null when no depth data was stored.

input_width.title: Input Width
input_width.description: Width in pixels of the submitted image.

input_height.title: Input Height
input_height.description: Height in pixels of the submitted image.

input_mode.title: Input Mode
input_mode.description: PIL mode of the submitted image (e.g. RGB, L, RGBA).

width.title: Width
width.description: Width in pixels of the produced depth map.

height.title: Height
height.description: Height in pixels of the produced depth map.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: PIL mode of the produced depth PNG (typically L).

array_shape.title: Array Shape
array_shape.description: Shape of the raw depth array stored in the NPZ (rows, cols).

min_depth.title: Min Depth
min_depth.description: Minimum depth value in the raw array, in meters.

max_depth.title: Max Depth
max_depth.description: Maximum depth value in the raw array, in meters.

params.title: Params
params.description: Resolved parameters used for the run (model, quantize, etc.).

EstimateResponse.title: EstimateResponse
EstimateResponse.description: Capability-specific ``result`` for a succeeded depthpro estimate job.

type.description: Job type. Use this to interpret the capability-specific result payload.

params.title: Params
params.description: Request parameters captured for inspection and reproducibility. Secret or large media payloads may be omitted or redacted by endpoints.

id.description: In-memory job id. Jobs are cleared when the kiapi process restarts.

status.description: Job lifecycle state: queued, running, succeeded, failed, or canceled.

artifacts.title: Artifacts
artifacts.description: File ids produced by the job. Use GET /v1/files/{file_id} for metadata or /download for bytes.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message when status is failed; otherwise null.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the job was created.

started_at.title: Started At
started_at.description: Unix timestamp when the worker started the job, or null while queued.

finished_at.title: Finished At
finished_at.description: Unix timestamp when the job reached a terminal state, or null while queued/running.

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: Best-effort completion fraction in [0.0, 1.0]. Null means the job has not reported progress.

progress_label.title: Progress Label
progress_label.description: Short human-readable phase label such as queued, running, denoising, saving, or done.

total_s.title: Total S
total_s.description: Wall-clock depth-estimation time in seconds (model run only).
